items.prompt:
A student sends a file across a school network.

Path options
```
Chromebook -> Wi-Fi router -> school server -> teacher laptop
```
Show how the learner would identify at least one route or pathway through which information moves.
items.expected_evidence: identify at least one route or pathway through which information moves
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates identify at least one route or pathway through which information moves with bounded and technically valid computing reasoning.

items.prompt:
A classroom app stops working because a login or system layer matters.

System note
```
App says: 'Cannot load assignment data'
Status page: network online, login token expired
```
Explain the setup and show how the learner would explain why a shared rule is needed for communication to work.
items.expected_evidence: explain why a shared rule is needed for communication to work
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain why a shared rule is needed for communication to work with bounded and technically valid computing reasoning.

items.prompt:
A security scenario involves access control and a possible attack.

Security brief
```
Shared club account password was posted in a group chat.
One student changed the page settings.
```
Show how the learner would distinguish a network path from one local device action.
items.expected_evidence: distinguish a network path from one local device action
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates distinguish a network path from one local device action with bounded and technically valid computing reasoning.

items.prompt: Why is it not enough to say devices are just connected?
items.expected_evidence: because a strong explanation shows how information moves through system parts and not just that a connection exists.
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to because a strong explanation shows how information moves through system parts and not just that a connection exists.

items.prompt: What is the purpose of a shared communication rule?
items.expected_evidence: a shared rule helps system parts send, receive, and interpret information in a compatible way.
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to a shared rule helps system parts send, receive, and interpret information in a compatible way.

items.prompt:
In a network or security scenario, a learner responds in a way that suggests this breakdown: learner treats network communication as unexplained appearance rather than movement through system parts.

Security artifact
```
Asset: shared class account
Event: password posted publicly
Result: settings changed by another user
```
Diagnose the systems or cybersecurity mistake. Focus on network-as-magic belief.
items.expected_evidence: identify at least one route or pathway through which information moves
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward identify at least one route or pathway through which information moves.

items.prompt:
In a network or security scenario, a learner responds in a way that suggests this breakdown: learner cannot explain why shared communication rules matter.

Security artifact
```
Asset: shared class account
Event: password posted publicly
Result: settings changed by another user
```
Diagnose the systems or cybersecurity mistake. Focus on protocol-purpose weakness.
items.expected_evidence: explain why a shared rule is needed for communication to work
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward explain why a shared rule is needed for communication to work.

items.prompt:
A network or security idea learned with classroom file sharing is now embedded in cloud storage.

Cloud case
```
Phone uploads photo -> cloud storage -> shared album -> friend views image
```
Explain how the learner would distinguish a network path from one local device action.
items.expected_evidence: distinguish a network path from one local device action
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves distinguish a network path from one local device action in a changed computing context or representation.

items.prompt:
A learner must move from a visible device-level description to a more abstract threat-model case.

Threat-model prompt
```
Asset: grades database
Threat: stolen staff password
Defense options: MFA, stronger password, unplug Wi-Fi
```
Show how they would avoid treating communication as if information appears instantly without system structure.
items.expected_evidence: avoid treating communication as if information appears instantly without system structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves avoid treating communication as if information appears instantly without system structure in a changed computing context or representation.

items.prompt:
After a delay, the learner is given a fresh connected-system or security case and must explain the pathway, layer, risk, or defense involved.

Delayed case
```
Student tablet -> hotspot -> video app -> streaming server
```
Show how they would identify at least one route or pathway through which information moves.
items.expected_evidence: identify at least one route or pathway through which information moves
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates identify at least one route or pathway through which information moves on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt:
Use this short systems or access-control artifact on connected devices and basic network awareness before closing network pathways, protocol ideas, and system dependence.

Probe artifact
```
Login page -> app dashboard -> cloud document -> share settings
```
Decide whether the current problem is actually an upstream dependency gap.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether connected devices and basic network awareness is secure enough to support network pathways, protocol ideas, and system dependence
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in connected devices and basic network awareness from a current-node issue in network pathways, protocol ideas, and system dependence.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: connected devices and basic network awareness

items.prompt:
Use this short systems or access-control artifact on data collection, classification, and simple visualization before closing network pathways, protocol ideas, and system dependence.

Probe artifact
```
Login page -> app dashboard -> cloud document -> share settings
```
Decide whether the current problem is actually an upstream dependency gap.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether data collection, classification, and simple visualization is secure enough to support network pathways, protocol ideas, and system dependence
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in data collection, classification, and simple visualization from a current-node issue in network pathways, protocol ideas, and system dependence.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: data collection, classification, and simple visualization
